calendar.matters:
NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Nov-25-2009 Off Calendar

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Reset To Nov-25-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Adult Abuse TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge: John Dearman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, CSR: Arlene Howell/CSR#2542, Bailiff: J. Capozzoli.

Richard Grabstein, Esq. for the Petitioner: Winifred Rawlings.

Bill Fazio, Esq. for the Respondent: Anne Cook.

Mr. Fazio stated that they were working diligently to resolve this matter. Mr. Grabstein stated that the parties were close to settlement and the if it is not resolved they will go forward at the next hearing. The Court continued the matter to November 25, 2009 at 11:00 a.m. in Department 204, and granted the reissuance (order signed).

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Sep-30-2009 Reset To Nov-04-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Adult Abuse TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge: John Dearman, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda/Geraldine Anderson, CSR: Arlene Howell/CSR#2542, Bailiff: J. Capozzoli.

Richard Grabstein, Esq. for the Petitioner: Winifred Rawlings.

Bill Fazio, Esq. for and with the Respondent: Anne Cook.

Outside the presence of the Court, Counsel confer regarding settlement.

At the initial call of the matter, Mr. Grabstein stated that the parties were going to continue to pursue their settlement, that he was in possession of a draft of the tentative agreement, requested that the matter be continued one month for additional discussions, and requested that the Temporary Restraining Order be reissued. Mr. Fazio concurred with Mr. Grabstein. The Court continued the matter to November 4, 2009 at 11:00 a.m. in Department 204, and granted the reissuance (order signed).

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Sep-09-2009 Reset To Sep-30-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Adult Abuse TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge Pro Temp: Sue Kaplan, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, CSR: Helen Sorep-Matley/CSR 225, Bailiff: J. Capazzoli.

Richard Grabstein, Esq. for the Petitioner: Winifred Rawlings, also present.

Bill Fazio, Esq. for the Respondent who was not present.

Parties were sworn.

Mr. Grabstein stated that his client and witnesses were present and they were ready to go. Mr. Fazio stated that his client was not present, that she is out of state, and that he was not prepared to go forward today. The matter was passed at the request of counsel. The parties confer outside the presence of the Court.

At the second call of the matter, Mr. Grabstein stated that the parties were going to continue to pursue a settlement, that the matter should go over a couple of weeks, that the Temporary Restraining Order should be reissued, and that he would not be inclined to agree to any further extensions. Mr. Fazio stated that he had just spoken to his client, that he should be able to settle this matter by the next date, and noted that his client informed him that if the matter did not settle she would not stipulate to the Commissioner hearing the matter.

The matter was continued to September 30, 2009. Mr. Grabstein is to prepare the reissuance.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Reset To Sep-09-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Grabstein for Winifred Rawlings (Petitioner). Bill Fazio for Anne Cook.

The Parties stipulated to a three week continuance. Mr. Fazio stated that they would continue to work towards a resolution. The matter was continued to September 9, 2009 at 11:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court signed the Request and Order for Reissuance of Temporary Restraining Order in open court which expires on September 9, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

NOTICE OF HEARING AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (ELDER OR DEPENDENT ADULT ABUSE)
Off Calendar Aug-05-2009 Reset To Aug-19-09 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 204 For Order To Show Cause
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Elder Abuse or Dependent Adult Abuse OSC and TRO hearing this day.

Dept. 204, Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, CSR: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Bailiff: Deputy K. Sandberg.

Richard Grabstein, Esq. for Plaintiff, Winifried Rawlings, also present.

Bill Fazio, Esq. for Defendant, Anne Cook, not present.

Both counsel stipulate to the Commissioner sitting as a Judge Pro Tem on the record.

Mr. Grabstein stated that the parties were appearing to request a continuance to allow Mr. Fazio time to respond. The matter was continued to August 19, 2009. The Court informed Mr. Fazio that if he filed a written response that it would like him to provide a courtesy copy to the Court.

Mr. Grabstein provided the Court with a reissuance which was signed in open.
